title: Fermented Gooey Argentinian Blackberry Empanadas
description: A traditional Argentinian dessert empanada with a gooey and fermented blackberry filling, made using Ayurvedic Rajasic-friendly methods.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine the blackberry jam, date syrup, and apple cider vinegar over low heat, stirring occasionally.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flaxseed meal and coconut oil to create a thick paste.
Add the yeast to the warm water and stir to dissolve.
In a separate bowl, combine the gluten-free flour, salt, and yeast mixture.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the blackberry jam mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and dehydrate at 50°C for 6 hours.
Preheat oven to 180°C and bake the empanadas for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
